body: Indonesia's Investment Minister Rosan Roeslani expects negotiations with Apple to resolve the ban on iPhone 16 sales in the country to be resolved within a week or two, following the company's failure to meet the 40% domestic component requirement. Apple has proposed a $1 billion investment to build an AirTag accessories factory in Batam, but this plan does not lift the ban on iPhone 16 sales, which requires the company to fulfill the domestic component requirement.
title: Indonesia's Investment Minister Expects Resolution to iPhone 16 Sales Ban Within Weeks

body: State-Owned Enterprises (SOEs) have contributed significantly to Indonesia's state revenue, with non-tax state revenue from separated state assets managed by SOEs reaching IDR 86.4 trillion in 2024, a 5.3% increase from IDR 82.1 trillion in 2023. From 2020 to 2023, the total fiscal contribution of SOEs to the state reached IDR 1,940 trillion, highlighting their growing role in Indonesia's economic development.
title: Minister of State-Owned Enterprises Erick Thohir highlights significant contributions of SOEs to Indonesia's state revenue through tax payments, non-tax revenue, and dividends.

body: Xiamen Yan Palace Bird's Nest Industry Co Ltd acquired a 5% stake in PT Esta Indonesia Tbk (ESTA) for IDR8.04 billion, increasing its share portion from 4.8% to 5% and boosting the company's business growth prospects. The investment is expected to support the company's sustainability, with PT Tunas Esta Indonesia, a subsidiary of PT Esta Indonesia Tbk (ESTA), planning to increase its swallow's nest factory capacity to 35 tonnes per year by 2026.
title: Yan Palace Acquires 5% Stake in PT Esta Indonesia to Boost Swallow's Nest Business Growth

body: President Prabowo Subianto issued a presidential instruction to review and cut budgets to save IDR 306.69 trillion, equivalent to 8.47% of the 2025 state budget of IDR 3,621.3 trillion. The instruction aims to improve efficiency, with the Finance Minister Sri Mulyani tasked with determining spending cuts for each ministry and institution, and adjusting regional transfer allocations.
title: President Prabowo Subianto Issues Instruction to Review and Cut State and Regional Budgets for Efficiency

body: Bank Indonesia Governor Perry Warjiyo highlighted three key aspects for investors to consider in response to global economic and financial market developments, citing uncertainty and volatility due to factors such as slowing global growth, supply chain dynamics, and high US government bond yields. Indonesia, with its strong economic fundamentals, offers various investment options, including Bank Indonesia securities, forex securities, and forex sukuk, making it an attractive destination for global investors.
title: Bank Indonesia Governor Highlights Three Key Aspects for Responding to Global Economic and Financial Market Developments.

body: Standard Chartered has revised its growth projection for Indonesia's gross domestic product (GDP) from 5.1% to 5% due to global challenges, but remains optimistic about the country's economic growth, citing strong domestic consumption and ongoing infrastructure constructions. The bank projects global economic growth at 3.1% in 2025, slightly lower than 3.2% in 2024, due to stricter monetary conditions and narrowing external demands, but believes Indonesia's solid domestic economy will help it navigate global challenges.
title: Standard Chartered revises Indonesia's GDP growth projection to 5% due to global challenges, but remains optimistic about domestic economic growth.

body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BBNI) reported a 11.6% year-over-year increase in loans to IDR 775.9 trillion, but net interest income declined 1.9% due to rising interest expenses, resulting in a modest 2.7% growth in net profit to IDR 21.7 trillion. Meanwhile, the bank's third-party funds decreased 0.6% to IDR 805.5 trillion, and its shares slipped 0.43% to IDR 4,650 as of Friday morning, despite rising 0.87% since the beginning of January.
title: BNI posts strong credit growth but sees slight decline in net interest income due to rising interest expenses.

body: PT Remala Abadi TBK (REM) saw a 12% decrease in its shares to IDR 1,685 from IDR 1,910 in the morning session, following a temporary suspension of its trading session by the Indonesia Stock Exchange due to a significant cumulative price jump. The suspension was intended to protect investors and serve as a "cooling-down" session, according to Yulianto Aji Sadono, Head of Transaction Supervision Division at the Indonesia Stock Exchange.
title: PT Remala Abadi's shares experience volatility on the Indonesia Stock Exchange after a temporary suspension.

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange has temporarily suspended trading of Link's (LINK) shares due to a significant cumulative price increase, with the stock closing at IDR 2,310, up 24.52% from the previous day. The suspension allows market players to carefully consider their investment decisions, following news that Axiata Malaysia is exploring the sale of its portion of Link for USD 1 billion.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Temporarily Suspends Trading of Link's Shares Due to Significant Price Increase.

body: The Indonesian government's new policy requiring 100% of foreign exchange gains from natural resource exports to be parked within the country for one year is expected to positively impact the rupiah's trend against the US dollar, similar to a strategy previously implemented in Thailand. The government has prepared incentives, including 0% tax on interest income and attractive deposit yields, to support the policy's implementation, which is set to take effect on March 1, 2025.
title: Indonesian government's new policy requiring 100% foreign exchange gains from natural resource exports to be parked within the country for one year expected to boost rupiah's value.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) saw a positive shift, led by PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk, and PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk, contributing a combined 16.32 index points. However, conglomerates' shares underperformed, with PT Pantai Indah Kapuk Dua Tbk, PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk, and PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk being the top laggards, dragging the index down by 23.92 points.
title: Jakarta Composite Index sees mixed performance with top gainers in banking and energy sectors, while conglomerates' shares decline.

body: Foreign investors bought a net 394.84 million shares on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on Thursday, January 23, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) leading the net buy list with 545.47 million shares, followed by PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with 48.75 million shares. On the other hand, PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) was the most sold share, with a net sell of 73.04 million shares, followed by PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) with 32.69 million shares.
title: Foreign investors buy 394.84 million shares in Indonesia Stock Exchange, led by Goto shares, on Thursday, January 23.

body: Sabana Prawirawidjaja spent IDR 541.42 billion to increase his stake in PT Campina Ice Cream Industry Tbk (CAMP), raising his shareholding to 4.94 billion shares, or 83.94% of the company's 5.88 billion listed shares. The transaction, carried out on December 27, 2024, nearly doubled Prawirawidjaja's stake from 43.94% previously, leaving a free float of 9.47% or 557.52 million shares.
title: Sabana Prawirawidjaja increases stake in PT Campina Ice Cream Industry to 83.94% through IDR 541.42 billion transaction.

body: PT Cemindo Gemilang Tbk (CMNT) has secured a USD 1.05 billion bank facility, equivalent to IDR 17.14 trillion, from Bangkok Bank Public Company Limited and PT Bank Permata Tbk, consisting of four facilities with varying tenors. The loan facility is expected to have a positive impact on the company's financial condition, supporting its future development, and consolidates existing loan facilities into a single, more advantageous financing structure.
title: PT Cemindo Gemilang secures USD 1.05 billion bank facility to consolidate existing loans and support business development.

body: PT Hilcon TBK (HILL) will undergo a 1:5 stock split, where one share will be divided into five new shares, with each share valued at IDR 20, increasing the total number of shares from 2.94 billion to 14.74 billion. The stock split is pending shareholder approval on March 3, 2025, and as of yesterday's closing bell, HILL's share price stood at IDR 2,140, with a transaction volume of 381,000 shares worth IDR 815.37 million.
title: PT Hilcon to Conduct 1:5 Stock Split, Increasing Total Shares to 14.74 Billion.

body: The Indonesian government will require 100% of foreign exchange gains from natural resource exports to be parked in Indonesia for one year to sustain national economic stability and resilience, with a new regulation being prepared in coordination with Bank Indonesia and the Financial Services Authority. The government is also offering a 0% income tax facility on interest revenue from these deposits and allowing exporters to use foreign exchange placement instruments as collateral for bank loans without affecting their debt-to-equity ratio.
title: Indonesia to require exporters to keep 100% of foreign exchange gains from natural resources in the country for one year to boost economic stability.

body: PT Brigit Biofarmaka Teknologi TBK (OBAT) expects significant revenue growth after acquiring a patent for its carbon absorber, Treealgae, which adopts carbon-absorbing technology to reduce emissions. The company, which recently debuted on the stock exchange with an IPO of IDR 59.5 billion, reported IDR 54.75 billion in revenue and IDR 12.64 billion in net profit in H1 2024, a significant increase from the same period in 2023.
title: PT Brigit Biofarmaka Teknologi Secures Patent for Carbon Absorber, Expects Significant Revenue Growth.

body: Star Energy Geothermal, a subsidiary of PT Barito Renewables Energy TBK, has collaborated with SLB (SLB) to develop geothermal assets, aiming to reduce risk and boost project efficiency through technology innovation. The partnership is expected to significantly improve efficiency and productivity, with Star Energy Geothermal currently having a total geothermal-based electricity capacity of 886 MW and expanding its efforts to promote energy transition.
title: Star Energy Geothermal partners with SLB to develop innovative geothermal technology solutions.

body: PT XL Axiata Tbk (EXCL) and PT Indosat Tbk (ISAT) saw a positive shift in their stock prices after being included in the IDX30 constituents list, with EXCL's free float shares reaching 33.29% of its total listed shares and ISAT's reaching 16.36%. As of 9:30 am WIB, EXCL's shares climbed to IDR 2,300 and ISAT's shares reached IDR 2,330 per share, up from IDR 2,280 and IDR 2,300 respectively at yesterday's closing bell.
title: Telco Stocks XL Axiata and Indosat Rise Following Inclusion in IDX30 Constituents List
